channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: The Impact and Risks of Political Cartooning on Society and Power
item.description: Political cartoonists have historically played a crucial role in critiquing those in power, often at significant personal risk. From Thomas Nast’s influential depictions of U.S. political parties to D...

item.title: Heatwave Alert Issued for 64 Mandals Across Andhra Pradesh
item.description: The Andhra Pradesh State Disaster Management Authority (APSDMA) has issued a warning regarding heatwave conditions expected in 64 mandals across the state. The affected districts include Vizianagaram,...

item.title: Kenya Eases Fuel Standards to Ensure Steady Supply Amid Global Disruptions
item.description: The Kenyan government has approved a temporary adjustment of fuel standards to guarantee a steady fuel supply following global disruptions. This decision was made after consultations between the Minis...

item.title: South Africa’s trade surplus dips in March despite stronger exports
item.description: South Africa recorded a preliminary trade surplus of R31.9 billion in March, reflecting a decline from the revised R35.9 billion surplus reported in February, according to the South African Revenue Se...

item.title: Terrafirma ends losing streak with strong fourth-quarter performance against Macau
item.description: In a much-needed victory, Terrafirma Dyip managed to halt their losing streak by defeating the Macau Black Knights in the fourth quarter of their PBA Commissioner's Cup game. The team displayed a resi...

item.title: Spain’s migrant amnesty sparks debate amid administrative strain and public concern
item.description: The article reports on tensions and controversy surrounding the Spanish government’s decision to grant temporary residency permits to up to 500,000 undocumented migrants. The policy, introduced by Pri...

item.title: Matt Wuerker’s Political Cartoons for May 2026
item.description: This Politico page presents a collection of editorial cartoons by Matt Wuerker for May 2026. Rather than telling one traditional news story, the page serves as a gallery of political commentary throug...

item.title: Tanzania Government Allocates 103.6 Billion Shillings to Expand National ICT Backbone Infrastructure
item.description: The Tanzanian government has set aside 103.6 billion shillings in the 2026/2027 fiscal year budget for the construction and expansion of the National ICT Backbone. This important infrastructure serves...

item.title: Inflation, Housing Market Shifts and ATO Tax Advice for Australians
item.description: This week, Australians are facing rising costs across several fronts. Inflation surged to 4.6 per cent in March, well above the Reserve Bank of Australia's target of 2–3 per cent, largely driven by a ...

item.title: Korean Air suspends transport of roosters on US-Philippine flights over animal welfare concerns
item.description: Korean Air has announced a suspension of transporting roosters of all ages on flights from the United States to the Philippines. This decision comes after sustained campaigns by animal rights activist...

item.title: Iran offers new peace proposal amid ongoing Israel-Hezbollah clashes
item.description: Iran has presented its latest peace proposal as the Israel-Hezbollah conflict shows no signs of abating. The proposal, which seeks to de-escalate the violence in the Middle East, comes amid escalating...

item.title: Bahati explains preference for Diana Marua’s natural and older look amid cosmetic debate
item.description: Kenyan musician Bahati has once again stirred conversation online after openly sharing his preference for his wife Diana Marua’s earlier, more natural look. This came after Diana posted throwback (TBT...

item.title: Cassie Carballo confirms return for final UAAP season, three teammates still deciding
item.description: In a significant development for the University of Santo Tomas (UST) women's volleyball program, setter Cassie Carballo has officially confirmed her decision to return for her final playing year in th...

item.title: Mumbai Press Club shares documents with NIA regarding Bhima Koregaon accused gathering
item.description: The National Investigation Agency (NIA) on Friday approached the Mumbai Press Club for documents concerning a social gathering held on January 19, which was attended by several persons accused in the ...

item.title: New SARS Commissioner Outlines Reform Agenda and Commitment to Fair Tax System
item.description: The South African Revenue Service (SARS) marked Workers' Day by recognising the contribution of the country’s workforce while introducing its new Commissioner, Dr Johnstone Makhubu, who officially beg...

item.title: US President to Raise EU Car and Truck Tariffs to 25% Over Trade Dispute
item.description: US President Donald Trump announced that tariffs on cars and trucks imported from the European Union will rise to 25% next week, up from the previously agreed 15%, citing the EU's slow compliance with...

item.title: US Embassy Issues Warning for Americans on Risky Areas in the Philippines After Toboso Killings
item.description: The US Embassy in Manila issued a security alert on May 1, 2026, urging American citizens to avoid mountainous and rural areas in regions like Negros, Samar, Leyte, and Mindoro due to concerns over th...

item.title: Georgia Toffolo enjoys a countryside weekend away following personal and business stress
item.description: Georgia Toffolo, the former Made In Chelsea star, and her husband James Watt have taken a brief countryside break at Heckfield Place, a five-star luxury hotel in Hampshire, over the recent bank holida...

item.title: Daniel Hall's Penalty Sends Auckland into Semi-finals
item.description: Auckland has secured a spot in the semi-finals thanks to a decisive penalty by Daniel Hall, who outperformed goalkeeper Patrick Beach. The match, which unfolded on May 2, 2026, saw Auckland progress a...

item.title: Nova Scotia Community Holds Rally on One-Year Anniversary of Missing Children
item.description: A Nova Scotia community is coming together to mark the first anniversary of the disappearance of siblings Lilly and Jack Sullivan, who went missing from their home in Lansdowne Station, Pictou County,...
